patches.find: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters (disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar), and the "confirmation" consists of matching that derivation to a globe-measured value — which, because the dome parameters were themselves fit to globe data (see Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b), is circular. There is no pre-registration of the derivation in a venue that timestamps independently of the author, and the dome monitor script (where one exists) encodes the expected value in the same commit as the comparison. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is retrodiction, not prediction.
patches.replace: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim fails our prediction test via a stronger failure mode than ordinary post-hoc retrodiction. The kernel the dome gets right is the theoretical posture: a self-consistent model should derive observables from its own geometry, and the H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) construction is genuinely sophisticated curve-fitting that unifies Schumann (~9,500 km), Polaris (~4,750 km) and model-average (~9,086 km) firmament heights into one exponential. The execution, however, never closes the loop. Having built H(r), the dome needed φ_sun(t) = f(H_sun, r_obs, r_sun(t), H(r), disc parameters); instead it substitutes the textbook globe formula φ_sun(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t − 81)/365) verbatim. The 23.45° is not a derived dome quantity. It is Earth's axial obliquity (J2000.0: 23.4393°), and the dome's V9 coordinate system treats it as a direct numerical input: lat_S = 90 − noon_altitude_june + 23.44 (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt, line 25). The functional form is also borrowed — the sinusoidal seasonal approximation is standard solar engineering (Duffie & Beckman 1980, ~46 years before WIN-056 was claimed), valid because Earth's spin axis is tilted relative to its orbital plane around a distant Sun. A flat disc with a local sun has no axial tilt and no orbital plane; the dome's own geometry produces θ = arctan(H_sun/d_obs), an arctangent in observer distance, not a sinusoid in day-of-year. So this is not derive-then-match — it is direct parameter import with form-substitution: the dome takes the globe's 23.45°, the globe's sinusoidal functional form, and the globe's geometric justification, and relabels the origin as a property of dome geometry. A defender will note that every model needs inputs, and we grant that; but an input that arrives wholesale from the rival model, together with the rival model's derivation, is not a dome prediction in any sense — it is transcription. The dome could close the loop by specifying sun motion within the cavity, deriving the resulting elevation-vs-latitude curve from dome parameters alone, and computing the sinusoidal amplitude (if any) without reference to 23.45°. Until that derivation exists, WIN-056 is a relabeling, not a derivation. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is not prediction.
patches.rationale: EXP-330: replaces generic post-hoc rider in WIN-056 with WIN-056-specific direct-import-with-form-substitution rebuttal. Curmudgeon WIN-056.c2 derives_from_dome=false; the rider was template boilerplate that contradicted the code analysis. Opening sentence and closing dome-author-definition + Part 3.1b cross-reference preserved verbatim. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged.
